text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
FoundationOne CDx
Foundation Medicine, Inc.

---

PATIENT INFORMATION
Patient Name: Margaret Yuen
Date of Birth: [on file]
Sex: Female
Diagnosis (Submitted): Non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma -- recurrent/progressing, pre-clinical trial biopsy
Primary Site: Lung, right upper lobe
ECOG Performance Status: 2
Ordering Physician: [Attending Oncologist, Medical Oncology]
Institution: [Medical Center, Department of Oncology]

SPECIMEN INFORMATION
Specimen ID: [Laboratory Accession Number on file]
Specimen Type: Formalin-Fixed, Paraffin-Embedded (FFPE) tissue
Procedure: CT-guided core needle biopsy, right upper lobe mass
Tissue Site: Right upper lobe primary tumor (repeat biopsy for clinical trial eligibility)
Tumor Purity (Estimated): 45% (adequate for analysis)
Pathologic Diagnosis on Submitted Specimen: Invasive pulmonary adenocarcinoma, acinar-predominant pattern; no small cell transformation; no squamous differentiation; TTF-1 positive. (See corresponding anatomic pathology report.)
Date of Procedure: [see corresponding pathology report]

ASSAY INFORMATION
Platform: FoundationOne CDx (F1CDx)
Genes Analyzed: 324 genes (full coding exons); select introns and gene fusions assessed
Sequencing Approach: Hybrid capture-based next-generation sequencing (NGS), tumor tissue only (no matched germline)
Mean Tumor Coverage: >500x
Additional Biomarkers: Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB), Microsatellite Instability (MSI)
Regulatory Status: FDA-approved in vitro diagnostic for detection of alterations in NSCLC and other solid tumors; companion diagnostic for multiple approved therapies

---

GENOMIC FINDINGS SUMMARY

CLINICALLY RELEVANT ALTERATIONS (TIER I / TIER II -- ACTIONABLE OR LIKELY ACTIONABLE)

1. EGFR p.L858R (Exon 21, missense substitution)
   - Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 48%
   - Classification: Activating / Sensitizing mutation (Tier I -- established clinical significance)
   - Interpretation: Classic EGFR sensitizing mutation (exon 21 L858R point mutation). This alteration is the founding oncogenic driver identified at initial diagnosis in this patient. The high VAF (48%) in the current tissue specimen, increased substantially from the ctDNA VAF of 3.8% detected on prior liquid biopsy, reflects enrichment of the EGFR-mutant clone in the setting of acquired therapeutic resistance. This mutation confers sensitivity to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs) including Tagrisso (Tagrisso), Tarceva, Iressa, and afat. The patient has already received first-line Tagrisso with a durable partial response followed by acquired resistance; L858R confirmation is required for eligibility on the planned clinical trial.

2. EGFR p.T790M (Exon 20, missense substitution, c.2369C>T)
   - Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 22%
   - Classification: Acquired resistance mutation (Tier I -- established clinical significance in the context of prior EGFR TKI therapy)
   - Interpretation: The gatekeeper T790M substitution confers resistance to first-generation (Tarceva, gefi) and second-generation (Gilotrif, daco) EGFR TKIs by steric hindrance of inhibitor binding and restoration of ATP affinity. In this patient, T790M was first detected at low VAF (1.6%) on prior plasma ctDNA analysis at the time of first progression on Tagrisso. In the current tissue biopsy, T790M VAF has risen to 22%, co-occurring with the primary L858R sensitizing mutation. The emergence of T790M in the context of upfront third-generation Tagrisso (Tagrisso) therapy is biologically atypical, as osi retains potent activity against T790M-bearing tumors; it is postulated that the T790M clone identified here represents a pre-existing subclonal population that was selectively enriched under Tagrisso selective pressure, and/or that additional bypass resistance mechanisms (see MET amplification, below) have emerged concurrently. EGFR C797S, which would confer tertiary resistance to osi in the context of T790M, was NOT detected in this specimen.
   NOTE: The EGFR L858R and T790M mutations are confirmed to co-occur in cis based on read-level analysis consistent with a single resistant clonal population.

3. MET Amplification (Copy Number: 6.8; High-Level Amplification)
   - Classification: Acquired resistance mechanism / Actionable bypass track alteration (Tier I -- established clinical significance)
   - Interpretation: High-level MET gene amplification (copy number 6.8 by sequencing-based copy number analysis) is detected in this specimen. MET amplification is one of the most clinically significant and well-characterized bypass resistance mechanisms to EGFR TKI therapy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, identified in approximately 5--20% of patients progressing on Tagrisso. Amplification of MET leads to activation of downstream RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT survival signaling independent of EGFR inhibition. MET amplification was NOT detected on prior plasma ctDNA analysis, suggesting either tissue-plasma discordance (lower ctDNA shedding of the MET-amplified subclone at that time) or subsequent clonal evolution and outgrowth. The detection of concurrent EGFR L858R/T790M mutations with high-level MET amplification defines a patient with multiple, potentially co-occurring, complementary resistance mechanisms. This combination of alterations is directly relevant to the planned clinical trial targeting both EGFR and MET via an investigational bispecific antibody approach. MET amplification in this degree (CN >6) is associated with responsiveness to MET-targeted strategies (e.g., tepotinib, capmatinib, savolitinib) and supports the bispecific EGFR/MET therapeutic hypothesis.

---

CO-MUTATIONS AND ADDITIONAL ALTERATIONS OF POTENTIAL CLINICAL RELEVANCE

4. TP53 p.R248W (Exon 7, missense substitution)
   - VAF: 52%
   - Classification: Likely Pathogenic / Co-mutation (Tier II)
   - Interpretation: TP53 R248W is a hotspot gain-of-function mutation at codon 248, one of the most frequently mutated residues in human cancer. This alteration has been present consistently across all prior molecular testing (initial tissue NGS VAF 38%; ctDNA VAF 5.2%; now tissue VAF 52%), confirming its status as an early truncal event in this tumor's evolutionary history. TP53 co-mutation in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is associated with a modestly worse prognosis and, in some data sets, shorter duration of benefit from EGFR TKI therapy. The increasing VAF (38% --> 52% from initial diagnosis to second progression) likely reflects increasing clonal dominance in the context of therapeutic selection.

5. RB1 p.Q670* (Exon 20, nonsense mutation; c.2008C>T)
   - VAF: 18%
   - Classification: Likely Pathogenic (Tier II -- emerging clinical relevance)
   - Interpretation: Newly detected in this specimen (not identified on initial tissue NGS or prior ctDNA analysis). RB1 loss-of-function mutations in EGFR-mutant NSCLC have been associated with accelerated resistance to EGFR TKI therapy, phenotypic plasticity, and an increased risk of histologic transformation (including small cell transformation, which was not identified on the current pathology specimen but warrants continued surveillance). RB1 p.Q670* introduces a premature stop codon, likely resulting in a truncated, non-functional protein and/or nonsense-mediated decay. This new RB1 alteration may be contributing to the evolving resistance biology in this tumor. The relatively modest VAF (18%) suggests either a subclonal distribution or partial allelic loss. Correlation with RB1 immunohistochemistry may be informative.

6. ARID1A p.E1814* (Exon 20, nonsense mutation)
   - VAF: 24%
   - Classification: Variant of Uncertain Significance (Tier III) -- VAF increased from 18% (initial tissue) to 24% (current tissue)
   - Interpretation: ARID1A encodes a subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex. Loss-of-function mutations in ARID1A have been observed across multiple tumor types and may influence sensitivity to certain therapeutic agents (e.g., HDAC inhibitors, EZH2 inhibitors, immune checkpoint inhibitors via mismatch repair interactions). In the context of this patient's tumor, ARID1A E1814* was initially detected as a VUS with modest VAF; its clonal expansion over time (18% --> 24%) suggests it is not a benign variant. Clinical significance in EGFR-mutant NSCLC at this site remains incompletely defined. No established actionable therapy is available for this alteration in isolation.

---

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS

- MET Amplification: Copy Number 6.8 (High-Level) -- see above (actionable; primary acquired resistance mechanism)
- CDK4 Amplification: Copy Number 3.8 (Low-level amplification)
  - Interpretation: CDK4 amplification detected at low level, potentially contributing to cell cycle dysregulation. CDK4/6 inhibitors (e.g., palbo, ribo, abema) are not currently standard of care in NSCLC, though emerging data suggest potential activity in CDK4/6-altered tumors. Noted as potentially relevant for future consideration.
- CCNE1 Amplification: Copy Number 4.1 (Low-level amplification)
  - Interpretation: CCNE1 (Cyclin E1) amplification is associated with CDK2 dependence and has been linked to resistance to CDK4/6 inhibitors and some chemotherapy regimens. Novel CDK2 inhibitors are under clinical investigation. Clinical relevance in this patient's context is currently investigational.
- CDK6 Copy Number Gain: Copy Number 2.8 (Low-level gain, consistent with prior testing)
  - Interpretation: Low-level CDK6 gain, previously identified at initial NGS. Stable. No established therapeutic implication at current copy number.

---

GENE FUSIONS / REARRANGEMENTS

- ALK fusions: NOT DETECTED
- ROS1 fusions: NOT DETECTED
- RET fusions: NOT DETECTED
- NTRK1 fusions: NOT DETECTED
- NTRK2 fusions: NOT DETECTED
- NTRK3 fusions: NOT DETECTED
- MET exon 14 skipping mutation: NOT DETECTED
- EGFR-related fusions: NOT DETECTED
- NRG1 fusions: NOT DETECTED
- BRAF fusions: NOT DETECTED

Note: No gene fusions or structural rearrangements of established clinical significance were identified in this specimen.

---

SELECT NEGATIVE FINDINGS OF CLINICAL IMPORTANCE

- EGFR Exon 20 p.C797S: NOT DETECTED (excludes Tagrisso tertiary resistance via C797S in the context of T790M)
- EGFR Exon 20 Insertions: NOT DETECTED
- EGFR Exon 19 Deletions: NOT DETECTED
- KRAS Mutations: NOT DETECTED (consistent with EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma biology; EGFR and KRAS mutations are nearly mutually exclusive)
- BRAF p.V600E: NOT DETECTED
- PIK3CA Mutations: NOT DETECTED
- ERBB2 (HER2) Amplification: NOT DETECTED (copy number 2.1, within diploid range)
- ERBB2 Mutations: NOT DETECTED
- STK11 Mutations: NOT DETECTED
- KEAP1 Mutations: NOT DETECTED
- SMAD4 Mutations: NOT DETECTED
- RB1 Exon 1-19 (remainder of gene): No additional alterations detected beyond p.Q670*

---

TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)

TMB: 8.4 mutations/megabase (mut/Mb)
Classification: INTERMEDIATE-LOW (TMB-Low by FDA-defined threshold of <10 mut/Mb)
Interpretation: TMB of 8.4 mut/Mb is intermediate-low and does not meet the threshold (>=10 mut/Mb) associated with a broad tumor-agnostic Keytruda (Keytruda) indication per the FDA label. This TMB level is above the patient's prior baseline (6.2 mut/Mb at initial tissue NGS), suggesting modest mutational evolution with disease progression; however, the increase is not of a magnitude to reclassify therapeutic eligibility. In the context of EGFR-mutant NSCLC, checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy has historically demonstrated limited efficacy, and the modest increase in TMB does not alter this interpretation. PD-L1 TPS on the concurrent pathology specimen is 28% (increased from 15% at initial diagnosis), which may modestly influence immunotherapy considerations within the context of combination regimens under investigation.

---

MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI)

MSI Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Interpretation: Microsatellite stable. No evidence of deficient mismatch repair (dMMR) by sequencing-based MSI analysis. MSI-H/dMMR immunotherapy indications do not apply.

---

GERMLINE FINDINGS DISCLAIMER
This is a tumor-only assay. Germline variants cannot be definitively distinguished from somatic variants in the absence of a matched normal sample. Variants with high population frequency (>1% minor allele frequency in population databases) and low VAF consistent with heterozygous germline status were excluded from reporting per standard filtering. Variants reported herein are interpreted in the somatic context. Germline genetic counseling referral may be considered for variants of potential hereditary significance if clinically indicated.

---

GENOMIC INTERPRETATION AND SUMMARY

This comprehensive genomic profiling report (FoundationOne CDx) was performed on a repeat CT-guided core needle biopsy of the right upper lobe primary tumor obtained at the time of second systemic disease progression, for the purpose of resistance mechanism characterization and clinical trial eligibility confirmation.

Key findings:

1. EGFR L858R (VAF 48%) is confirmed as the founding oncogenic driver, present at high clonal frequency. This mutation is required for enrollment on the planned EGFR/MET bispecific antibody clinical trial and is consistent with EGFR-addicted NSCLC biology.

2. EGFR T790M (VAF 22%) is a co-occurring acquired resistance mutation, now detected at substantially higher tissue VAF than on prior ctDNA analysis (1.6%). The co-occurrence of L858R and T790M in cis constitutes a resistant EGFR compound mutation. Although third-generation osi retains activity against T790M in the first-line setting, the emergence of T790M alongside high-level MET amplification under osi selective pressure defines a particularly resistant tumor profile.

3. MET amplification (CN 6.8, high-level) is the dominant acquired bypass resistance mechanism in this tumor. MET-driven bypass signaling sustains downstream oncogenic signaling despite EGFR pathway inhibition. This finding is directly targetable by the planned investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody and represents the primary molecular rationale for clinical trial enrollment. High-level MET amplification (CN >6) is a well-established predictor of benefit from MET-targeted therapy in this context.

4. RB1 p.Q670* (VAF 18%, newly detected) raises concern for emerging phenotypic plasticity and possible histologic transformation potential, although no small cell transformation was identified on current pathology. Continued clinical and radiographic surveillance is warranted.

5. TP53 p.R248W (VAF 52%) is a persistent, high-frequency truncal co-mutation associated with modestly adverse prognosis in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

6. ARID1A p.E1814* (VAF 24%) remains a VUS with increasing clonal frequency; no immediately actionable implication.

Overall genomic profile: EGFR L858R/T790M co-mutation with high-level MET amplification as a combined, co-occurring acquired resistance mechanism, arising in the setting of prior osi and subsequent platinum-based chemotherapy. This genomic landscape is clinically consistent with the EGFR/MET co-targeting therapeutic hypothesis and supports enrollment on the planned investigational bispecific antibody clinical trial.

---

QUALITY METRICS
Percent Tumor Nuclei (submitted estimate): 45%
Mean Exon Coverage: 512x
Uniformity of Coverage: 98.6%
Percent Exons >= 100x: 99.1%
Low-Quality Variants Filtered: Yes (per standard F1CDx QC pipeline)
Assay Result: REPORTABLE (all quality thresholds met)

---

Laboratory Director: [Name on file]
CAP Accreditation Number: [On file]
